session_name_by_week.14: RAG, Vectors, Agents and Production AI

narrative_seed_by_week.14: Vector databases — store embeddings, enable similarity search at scale / Retrieval pipeline — query → embed → similarity search → top-k → inject into prompt / Why RAG reduces hallucination — AI answers from retrieved evidence, not from memory / When to use RAG — use it when the answer depends on data the model was not trained on / Agent anatomy — LLM + memory + tools + planning loop; the ReAct pattern / Agent vs simpler flow — decision matrix: direct call → chained calls → agent; use the simplest that works / When NOT to use agents — high-stakes or irreversible actions require human-in-the-loop / Production AI patterns — cost, latency, reliability trade-offs when RAG and agents are in the loop / Note: RAG build and agent implementation are Semester 2 skills — Semester 2 Week 14 goes deeper with implementation

lab_activity_seed_by_week.14: Live demo 1 — RAG over a domain FAQ: with vs without retrieval, accuracy compared live / Live demo 2 — agent completing a multi-step task in real time / For your domain system: does it need RAG or an agent? Write a one-paragraph architectural decision with justification using the decision matrix / Journal Entry #9 — what was surprising about how RAG or agents worked in the demos?
